mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates lower the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate declines are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate crossing above 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration, reducing household income growth and corporate earnings expectations, which drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of monetary easing reflects accommodative financing boosting manufacturing output, leading to short-term market rallies as supply constraints ease.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following industrial production increases are negative or flat during easing cycles, or the signal reverses direction when rate cuts exceed 50 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive forward equity returns over quarterly horizons as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.0 percent fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the correlation between GDP revisions and forward returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index breaching 240.0 triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of instances when CPI exceeds 240, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations remain anchored below 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting consumer spending and equity valuations through higher wage growth expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to generate positive 20-day returns in over 55 percent of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during late-cycle phases signal weakening consumer balance sheets and reduced construction activity, forecasting negative short-term market returns as demand contracts.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following housing starts declines are positive in more than 60 percent of late-cycle instances, or the signal reverses when mortgage rates fall below 3.5 percent.
